record.field:
# WARNING: This bypasses all accounting constraints and audit trail protections # Use only for removing test/erroneous data if records: move_ids = tuple(records.ids)
# Get all journal item IDs first (before deletion) env.cr.execute("SELECT id FROM account_move_line WHERE move_id IN %s", (move_ids,)) line_ids = tuple([row[0] for row in env.cr.fetchall()])
# Delete related records in correct order to avoid foreign key constraints if line_ids: # 1. Delete payment reconciliation links FIRST (they reference account_move_line) env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM account_partial_reconcile WHERE debit_move_id IN %s OR credit_move_id IN %s", (line_ids, line_ids))
# 2. Delete analytic lines (if any) env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM account_analytic_line WHERE move_line_id IN %s", (line_ids,))
# 3. Delete tax lines relationships env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM account_move_line_account_tax_rel WHERE account_move_line_id IN %s", (line_ids,))
# 4. Delete journal items (account.move.line) env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM account_move_line WHERE move_id IN %s", (move_ids,))
# 5. Delete mail messages (chatter/audit trail) env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM mail_message WHERE model = 'account.move' AND res_id IN %s", (move_ids,))
# 6. Delete mail followers env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM mail_followers WHERE res_model = 'account.move' AND res_id IN %s", (move_ids,))
# 7. Delete activity records env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM mail_activity WHERE res_model = 'account.move' AND res_id IN %s", (move_ids,))
# 8. Delete the journal entries themselves env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM account_move WHERE id IN %s", (move_ids,))

# WARNING: This bypasses all accounting constraints # Deleting individual journal items may leave entries unbalanced if records: line_ids = tuple(records.ids)
# Delete reconciliation links first env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM account_partial_reconcile WHERE debit_move_id IN %s OR credit_move_id IN %s", (line_ids, line_ids))
# Delete the journal items env.cr.execute("DELETE FROM account_move_line WHERE id IN %s", (line_ids,))
